title: Extraction and Characterization of Hemicellulose and Lignin Fractions from Red Oak and Douglas Fir Biomass
author: Katahira, Rui
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This technical report documents the isolation of hemicellulose and native-like lignin fractions from representative hardwood (Red Oak) and softwood (Douglas Fir) biomasses. Hemicellulose was extracted using a dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO)–based protocol designed to preserve acetyl and ester functionalities, while lignin was isolated via flow-through methanol solvolysis to maintain native structural features. 
 The extracted fractions were characterized using compositional analysis and spectroscopic techniques, including gel permeation chromatography and nuclear magnetic resonance spectroscopy, to verify chemical identity, molecular weight distribution, and structural integrity relative to the parent biomass. These materials were produced to support subsequent investigations of the thermal stability and decomposition behavior of isolated biomass components. 
 This report was produced by the National Renewable Energy Laboratory under U.S. Department of Energy contract and Fire Safety Research Institute sponsorship and is made publicly available in accordance with the governing agreement.
